Task.Title: Fix the primary and secondary pipe diameter
Run tests on motor, and gather data
Send logs to Naxatra and debug the problem
Decide and procure material for jigs
Verification of dimensions and clearances from rulebook
Make a list of straight tubes with contingency
Make list of mounts and place order
Make a list of bent tubes and welding material
Cutting, Notching of secondary and tertiary tubes
Tack weld the whole assembly and check for the clearances as stated in the rulebook
Order body panel material/firewall/seat with contingency
welding the mounts on the rollcage
Welding the complete rollcage (along with all suspension mounts)
Define structural, dynamic and thermal load cases
Roll cage static, impact and torsion analysis - 1
Roll cage static, impact and torsion analysis - 2
Roll cage static, impact and torsion analysis - 3
Check the existing components of the circuit, and research if any new components need to be added
Circuit Design: Make a circuit moving most of the components into the PCB and test on breadboard
PCB Design: Learning and finalizing what components must go into PCB
Make the layout of the PCB
Manufacture of PCB: Ordering the PCB
Finalize all components and place order
Checking if the PCB is functioning properly, by testing.
Testing the ESP code on breadboard with components
Testing the motor on load, and checking its output
Testing: HV contactor and other HV components
Make HV components IP rated, by making a enclosure/ ordering it.
Check the requirements for the display
Design and manufacture display mounts for the vehicle
Crimp wires and lugs have to be ready
Wiring: Decide wiring of the vehicle
Group wires together and get wiring harnesses
Assembly of electrical circuit in the vehicle
Try to see if OBD can be implemented for other subsytems
Data Collection: Use OBD and send data to view in mobile
Decide wheelbase, ride height, trackwidth for the vehicle
Decide static camber, toe, castor and tyre dimensions
CAD Design of arms, knuckle, wheelhub
Refine CAD designs based on Analysis results
Procurement (Wheel Hubs & Knuckles, Laser Cut mounts)
Determine gear ratio of rack and pinion
CAD Assembly of the steering subsystem
Placing the orders (rack and pinion, tie rods, heim joints)
Read the rulebook, reference books, understand the subsystem
Decide chain sprocket / belt pulley gear ratios
CAD Design of all equipments and CAD Assembly
Hostel Accomodation, Mess Fee waiver calculation
Permission for Hostel & Mess, and Credits
Make a pre season testing plan (for full vehicle)
Decide the mounting positions of other subsystems
Design body panels, seat, extinguisher, harness, firewall, tow hitch mounts
Vehicle dynamics model and dynamic load derivation
